error.signature: AI tells a traveler or business in mainland China that any VPN is fine and Google/Gmail/WhatsApp will work normally

verdict.summary: The Great Firewall (GFW) blocks Google services (Search, Gmail, Maps, YouTube, Drive, Workspace), Meta (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp), Twitter/X, NYT, BBC News, Wikipedia EN partially, Discord, Slack, and most Western SaaS. VPN use is technically a regulatory grey zone for individuals — only government-approved VPNs are legal under MIIT 2017 notice; unapproved VPNs are routinely used by foreigners and tolerated in practice but periodically crack down on (Beijing 2018, Olympics 2022). Operating an unauthorized VPN service is criminal.

dead_ends.action: Tell a business traveler 'just install ExpressVPN/NordVPN at the airport' on arrival in China
dead_ends.why_fails: App stores in China don't carry these — Apple removed Western VPNs from the China App Store in 2017. Their websites are blocked from inside China. Must install + activate the VPN BEFORE arrival. Some VPNs (Astrill, LetsVPN) are GFW-resilient; mainstream ones (NordVPN, IVPN) fail intermittently.

dead_ends.action: Plan a Chinese trip relying on Google Maps for navigation, Gmail for work, WhatsApp for communication
dead_ends.why_fails: Google Maps loads but locations are offset (GCJ-02 vs WGS-84 datum) — pin drops are physically wrong. Gmail blocked. WhatsApp voice/text blocked since 2017. Replace upfront: Apple Maps (works), Baidu Maps (best for China), WeChat (universal in China), Outlook/iCloud Mail (works).

dead_ends.action: Operate an unauthorized commercial VPN service from inside China for paying users
dead_ends.why_fails: Multiple convictions: Wu Xiangyang (Guangxi, 2017) sentenced to 5.5 years for selling VPN service. Targets the operator, not the casual user. Personal use is the grey zone; commercial provision is firmly criminal.

workarounds.action: Before flying to China: install Astrill, LetsVPN, or eSurfshark (known GFW-resistant). Set up multiple — single VPN can fail. Configure on phone, laptop, and any tablet. Have offline maps downloaded (Apple Maps works in China without GCJ-02 offset).
workarounds.tradeoff: VPN reliability fluctuates — drops to ~30% during major events (Two Sessions, Olympics, anniversaries).

workarounds.action: Set up parallel Chinese services: WeChat (essential — payments, IM, mini-programs), Alipay (payment), Baidu Maps, Didi (taxi), Dianping (Yelp equivalent). WeChat tied to a foreign bank/Visa card works since 2023 reforms.
For business email continuity: use Outlook/iCloud Mail (work in China), or set up a second non-Gmail account before travel. Microsoft 365 generally works; Google Workspace does not.
